description: This theme captures the surprise and realization of individuals realizing their initial perceptions or predictions about economic trends were incorrect or were not aligned with reality.
quotes: I was wrong. I thought everybody believed the economy was bad.
I may have overestimated how many Republicans think about the economy in a meaningful, grounded way.
My guess was not close to the actual averages.
I was wrong here actually.
I underestimated how Republicans view what is going on.
I guessed a downward trend but republicans reported an upward trend
My response was a higher guess with varied level. The actual results were lower and more consistent.
Another wrong guess
I am surprised that this amount was a high as it was
I genuinely find this one hard to believe
I thought the numbers would go down but the trend seems to be consistent or go up
I thought the opposite would be true.
Again it looks like I was wrong. I thought you would drop down to about 3% but they actually only dropped to 17 so I was completely wrong again.
I thought you would drop down to about 3% but they actually only dropped to 17 so I was completely wrong again.
I did pretty well in the middle part.
I was almost spot on.
I guessed 63 - 77 and republicans feel 63 - 73
My guess was lower than the confidence interval and the average response from republicans
I thought as 2026 was included it would be much lower.
Republican optimism persists despite contrasting economic indicators suggesting ongoing financial challenges.

description: This theme highlights the surprise at the level of approval or support for leadership, particularly the president, which was counter to personal expectations or predictions.
quotes: They think he is doing well for some reason.
Further consolidating opinions about the President
It must be because of who is president.
I wonder if the approval rate is still the same after the war.
They increased not decreased in this area
Unfortunate
General Disappointment
I have to say I don't believe this data given inflation, etc.
I don't believe the approval rate has increased
Seriously? It's increased???? I shouldn't be surprised but I am, honestly.
This one surprised me! I guessed their approval at a higher rate!
Numbers are better than I expected in the 60-70 range
This surprises me
I am shocked. I don't want to speak on the intelligence of another person, but I really am questioning it here.
I'm curious if the trend has gone down since the war with Iran began and fuel prices have been hugely impacted.
Majority feeling Trump is doing a good job as president
I simply cannot understand how Republicans could feel better about the economy right now.
Trump Derangement Syndrome is real
I guess they equate the stock market to the economy.
The support for the economy has largely been influenced by partisan loyalties rather than objective economic indicators.

description: This theme captures the disconnect between people's perceptions or expectations about the economy or approval ratings and the actual data or outcomes, highlighting surprise or disbelief.
quotes: I don't believe the approval rate has increased
I'm surprised that the Republicans think the economy was increasingly on the right track
This is baffling to me
The responses aren't within A straight line. This could indicate some hesitance
Because this is wildly out of touch with reality.
What a bizarre separation from reality
I don't see a clear pattern.
Based on these numbers, the Democrats seem to be out of touch.
It's like surface level support because they don't want to admit they were wrong
In November 2025, there was a noticeable dip but it quickly rebounded
A significant spike between Nov 2025 and Jan 2026
Again, a pretty steady trend which I find completely surprising.
I am extremely surprised to see that at any point the Democrat opinion of this topic has improved.
It shows the number has increased, with the final number being the highest on the chart.
The percentage remained relatively stable over the last 12 months
Republicans have slightly increased their belief that the economy was on the right track.
Democrats thought the economy was going down by a considerable amount.
Only a few have a positive thought on that.
Conversely, the guesses demonstrate the significant growth, which means that people highly overestimate the optimism and positive tendencies among Democrats.
Slightly more Republicans see the economy being on the right track now compared to a year ago.

description: This theme explores concerns and criticisms regarding the sample size and representativeness of data, questioning the validity and bias in collected responses.
quotes: 100 democrats seems to be a small sample, however, for Harvard?
Its only out of 100 people
Now we only have 100 respondents, data is very biased and unreliable.
Again, not happy with the 100 respondents.
The data is from only 100 respondents??? Is that enough to be meaningful?
I wonder where Harvard is polling people.
Is this even representative of the population
Is this really representative
This poll is trash
I don't think you can base much on 100 people
Poll shows distortion
Sample is not large enough to draw conclusions
Sample size is key
Sample size concerns me
Sample size worries me
Why base this on 100
100 respondents is not enough
Not enough responses
I'm unsure about sample size validity
Too few respondents
